news.headline: French Government spokeswoman, on Trump tariffs: We are ready in this trade war
French Government Spokeswoman: France will respond along with Europe to Trump tariffs, reaffirms could target digital services in reciprocal measures
French government Spokeswoman: We will continue to talk over Trump tariffs, there will likely be a first response in mid-April and then another one in late April
Lawmakers demand SEC disclose details of Trump's crypto ties due to conflict of interest concerns in enforcement cases.
news.summary: Lawmakers have expressed concern over potential conflicts of interest related to Donald Trump's ties to the cryptocurrency industry. They have called upon the SEC to provide clarity on paused enforcement cases. The lawmakers believe that this situation presents an unparalleled conflict of interest that must be addressed.

news.headline: Speculation swirls that ChatGPT may have sparked Trump's tariff strategy with a 10% levy on all nations. AI similarity in formula raises eyebrows. US imposes higher tariffs on certain countries like China (34%), Japan (24%), and EU (20%) on April 5th. Crypto markets dip 5% post announcement.
news.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a reciprocal tariff plan affecting all countries with a minimum 10% tariff. Social media users speculated it could have been designed by an AI chatbot as they were able to duplicate the plan using OpenAI’s ChatGPT. The AI-generated plan and Trump’s plan shared similarities, leading to speculations. The tariffs were to be effective from April 5, impacting all countries with varying rates - China at 34%, Japan at 24%, and the EU at 20%. The plan led to a 5% dip in the crypto market with Bitcoin falling to $82,277 before recovering slightly. Some experts reverse-engineered the tariff formula based on trade deficits and country exports. The usage of AI in geopolitics was also discussed, with some suggesting this as the first large-scale application.

news.headline: Norway Prime Minister: we will negotiate with the US on tariffs if given the opportunity to do so
Survey in India by CGAP and Sahamati shows growth in Account Aggregator adoption. 1,860 smartphone users surveyed.
news.summary: In a recent national survey by CGAP, the adoption and awareness of India's Account Aggregator (AA) ecosystem have been steadily increasing. The survey conducted in 2024 included 1,860 smartphone users from various regions in India in partnership with Sahamati. This study followed customer research conducted by CGAP in late 2023 to evaluate the understanding and adoption of the AA ecosystem among consumers.

news.headline: Credit analysts are changing roles due to the revival of the Chinese market, with a significant increase in job transfers.
Shoplazza introduces upgraded payment solution to increase e-commerce conversion rates globally.
news.summary: In a recent update on April 3, 2025, Shoplazza, a prominent e-commerce SaaS platform worldwide, unveiled a new payment system aimed at enhancing user checkout processes. This innovation is geared towards lowering cart abandonment rates and fostering faster expansion in global markets. This upgraded solution sets out to improve the overall shopping experience for merchants and customers alike, with a focus on international growth.

news.headline: Kia and LG Electronics unveil PBV concepts for work and play: PV5 Spielraum Studio for work and PV5 Spielraum Glow cabin for outdoor activities debut in 2025.
news.summary: Kia and LG Electronics have partnered to showcase unique concept models designed for versatile mobile environments using Kia's Platform Beyond Vehicles (PBVs). Two prototypes were introduced at the 2025 event: the PV5 Spielraum Studio catering to work scenarios and the PV5 Spielraum Glow cabin tailored for outdoor recreational activities. These innovative concepts aim to revolutionize professional as well as leisure spaces on the go, combining cutting-edge technology and forward-thinking design.

news.headline: Gold hits all-time high at $3,148.93 per ounce, up 20% YTD; Tether Gold and PAX Gold rise over 20% in 2020.
news.summary: The price of gold reached a new record high on Wednesday amid investor concerns over President Donald Trump's tariffs, leading to a downturn in risk-on markets. Spot gold saw a 0.46% increase to $3,148.93 per ounce, momentarily peaking at $3,167 earlier in the day before slightly retreating. Gold futures on the Commodity Exchange rose by 0.15% to $3,171. This surge pushed the precious metal's year-to-date gains to 20%. The uptick also had a positive impact on physical gold-backed cryptocurrencies: Tether Gold and PAX Gold saw a 1.02% rise in the past 24 hours and over 20% since the beginning of 2025. These two crypto assets derived from gold were among those affected.

news.headline: Bitcoin mailing list temporarily taken down by bot spam, likely a targeted attack on April 2, affecting developers. Issue resolved on April 3 at 2:23 am UTC. BTC network secures over $1.6 trillion. No plans to move from email communication. Bitcoin's white paper shared in 2008.
news.summary: The Bitcoin Development Mailing List, a communication platform for Bitcoin core developers, was banned by Google for several hours on April 2 after being labeled as containing spam or malware. This ban was potentially caused by a targeted attack involving mass reporting from individuals or bots. Bitcoin advocate and tech entrepreneur Jack Dorsey urged Google's CEO to investigate the issue. The Bitcoin mailing list is crucial for discussing protocol changes of Bitcoin, securing over $1.6 trillion worth of network value. Despite the incident, the moderators plan to continue using email as the primary communication method for the list. The platform migrated to Google Groups in February 2024 from the Linux Foundation's infrastructure. Multiple other platforms like GitHub and Nostr are also used for Bitcoin development discussions.

news.summary: On April 3, 2025, DNO ASA, a leading oil and gas operator based in Norway, unveiled its 2024 Annual Report, Accounts, Remuneration Report, and Annual Statement of Reserves and Resources. This critical release signifies the company's performance, financial status, executive compensation details, and assessment of reserves and resources for the mentioned year. news.headline: AS Tallinna Sadam's supervisory board approved audited 2024 annual report with no differences from interim report.
news.summary: The supervisory board of Tallinna Sadam has given approval to the audited annual report for the year 2024 as presented by the management board. In comparison to the unaudited interim report released on 28th February, there have been no variations in the audited financial outcomes for the year.

news.headline: Japan Prime Minister Ishiba: will not hesitate to communicate directly with Trump if needed
US grants orphan drug designation for Rilzabrutinib in 2 rare diseases with no approved medicines.
news.summary: Rilzabrutinib has received orphan drug designation in the United States for the treatment of two rare diseases that currently have no approved medications. This designation is granted to drugs intended for the treatment, diagnosis, or prevention of diseases or conditions that affect fewer than 200,000 individuals in the country. Orphan drug status provides the drug developers with various incentives and benefits, including tax credits for qualified clinical testing costs, exemptions from certain FDA fees, and seven years of market exclusivity upon regulatory approval. This designation underscores the significant unmet medical needs in the rare disease space that Rilzabrutinib aims to address.

Dow Jones Futures fall as market recovers ahead of imminent Trump tariffs. Market bounced back Monday.
news.summary: Stock market futures saw a notable rebound on Monday, with Dow Jones Futures indicating a bullish recovery, as investors brace for the impact of the upcoming Trump tariffs set to be implemented this week. The positive response in the market comes following a period of decline. The recovery is seen as a hopeful sign amidst uncertainties. The anticipation of the tariffs has prompted caution among investors, with the market eyeing the potential consequences. This development is closely monitored by analysts and experts to gauge the overall market sentiment and potential outcomes.

news.headline: Lucid Group, Inc. prices $1 billion convertible senior notes offering due 2030 in a private offering.
news.summary: Lucid Group, Inc. based in Newark, California, disclosed on April 3, 2025, that they have priced an offering of $1 billion in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2030. The offering, traded under Nasdaq as LCID, was conducted privately with individuals deemed to be qualified for participation.

news.headline: Counterfeit phones sold with $270,000 stolen crypto via Triada malware infecting Android devices. 2,600 infections worldwide. Stay vigilant.
news.summary: A cybersecurity firm, Kaspersky, revealed the discovery of thousands of counterfeit Android smartphones being sold online, containing preinstalled malware designed to steal sensitive data and cryptocurrencies. The Triada Trojan infected devices are being sold at reduced prices, with attackers having almost complete control over the compromised devices. The attackers have been able to transfer approximately $270,000 in cryptocurrencies to their wallets and are targeting Monero, an untraceable cryptocurrency. The trojan can also steal user account information, intercept texts, and bypass two-factor authentication. Kaspersky researchers have identified 2,600 confirmed infections in various countries, with Russia encountering the most cases in the first quarter of 2025. The malware, which first appeared in 2016, targets financial and messaging applications and can be delivered through malicious downloads and phishing campaigns. To protect against this threat, users are advised to purchase devices only from legitimate distributors and install security solutions immediately after purchase. Other cybersecurity firms have also reported new forms of malware targeting crypto users, including a fake overlay malware tricking Android users into providing their crypto seed phrases and a remote access trojan targeting crypto held in Google Chrome wallet extensions.

news.headline: Leading scholars attended 2025 PKU Shenzhen Forum on AI and Economic Development in China. Held at Wuzhou Guest House, Futian District on March 30.
news.summary: On April 3, 2025, the 2025 PKU Shenzhen Forum took place in Shenzhen, China, organized by Peking University HSBC Business School (PHBS). The event was held on March 30 at the Wuzhou Guest House in Futian District. With the theme "Building Consensus on Reform, Stimulating Innovation," the forum aimed to bring together experts and professionals to discuss important topics.

news.headline: Dogecoin drops 16% in a week, now at mid-$0.1600 after reaching $0.20. Open interest in DOGE futures fell 24%.
news.summary: The popular dog-themed cryptocurrency, Dogecoin (DOGE), experienced a decline following President Donald Trump's tariff announcement, resulting in a drop of over 16% for the week. The cryptocurrency, which had reached a high of $0.1787, fell to the mid-$0.1600 region in response to the new reciprocal tariff structure and the subsequent market downturn. This downfall marks a significant reversal from last week's peak of $0.20, reflecting a loss of over 16%. The price decrease led to a decrease in speculative interest, with Open Interest in DOGE futures dropping by 24% over the week.
